original_hypothesis.title: Blacklisted ADMIN Can Bypass Sender Blacklist and Pause via adminSanity Exemption
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: adminSanity() exempts ADMIN role holders from both pause check and sender blacklist check. A blacklisted admin can call transfer() and transferFrom() even when the contract is paused and they are blacklisted as sender. This means blacklisting an admin address does not prevent them from moving their own tokens.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: If an ADMIN account is compromised, the owner might blacklist the admin and pause the contract as emergency measures. However, the compromised admin can still transfer tokens out because adminSanity exempts admins from both checks. The owner must revoke ADMIN role first, creating a race condition.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (!hasRole(ADMIN, _msgSender())) { if (paused()) revert; if (isBlacklisted(from)) revert; } — ADMIN skips both pause AND sender blacklist

preconditions: ADMIN role is not yet revoked

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: ADMIN check bypasses entire block containing both pause and sender blacklist checks
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Blacklisting an admin has no effect on their ability to transfer as sender
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Blacklist should apply to all addresses regardless of role for outbound transfers

analysis_questions: Can the owner revoke ADMIN role before the admin can transfer tokens out?
Does the admin exemption also apply to transferWithAuthorization?
Is this intentional design for compliance force-transfer workflows?
validation_requirements: Test: blacklist admin, pause contract, admin calls transfer - verify it succeeds
Verify admin exemption applies to all three transfer functions
Check if recipient blacklist still applies (line 66)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: ADMIN account compromised. Owner blacklists admin and pauses contract. Compromised admin calls transfer(attacker_wallet, full_balance) - adminSanity exempts them from both pause and sender blacklist. Tokens moved before owner can revoke ADMIN role.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Compromised admin can move tokens despite blacklist/pause, limited to admin's own balance
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner must revoke ADMIN role as first action, not blacklist

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for blacklisted admin attempting standard transfers

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Single transaction race with owner's role revocation
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Transfer events from blacklisted address visible

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
